cards.back: A greedy algorithm used to find the minimum spanning tree of a connected, undirected graph. It works by selecting the minimum-weight edge from the graph and adding it to the tree, and repeating this process until the entire graph is covered.

A greedy algorithm used to find the minimum spanning tree of a connected, undirected graph. It works by sorting all the edges of the graph in non-decreasing order of their weights and then adding the smallest edge to the tree, and repeating this process until the entire graph is covered.

A greedy algorithm used to find the shortest path between two nodes in a weighted graph. It works by maintaining a priority queue of nodes to visit, and repeatedly selecting the node with the smallest distance from the source node.

A data structure used to compress data by assigning shorter codes to more frequently occurring characters. Huffman trees are constructed by building a tree where the leaf nodes represent characters and the edges represent the frequency of the characters.

A method of assigning binary codes to characters in a data set, where the codes are assigned based on the frequency of the characters. Huffman codes are used to compress data by replacing each character with a shorter code, resulting in a smaller compressed file size.
